macro_summary: Friday's session saw a sharp selloff after the May jobs report crushed expectations at 172K vs 85K estimated, forcing traders to fully price in a Fed rate hike by year-end. The Nasdaq plunged 4.12%, the VIX exploded 39.8% to 21.51, and the 10-year yield climbed 6bp to 4.54%. The selling compounded Broadcom's AI chip forecast miss from Thursday, dragging the SOXX semiconductor index down 8.71% in a broad-based rout.
commodities_note: WTI crude eased 2.8% to $90.54 as recession fears outweighed the ongoing Strait of Hormuz blockade, while gold fell 2.9% on a stronger dollar. The VIX spike signals acute risk aversion, with energy-adjacent assets caught between supply disruption and demand-destruction narratives.

singapore.summary: The rate-hike jolt pushed US10Y to 4.54%, deepening the NIM compression that has held all three SG banks in CAUTION for over 70 days. No divergence — all are extended or overbought, and the next MAS decision isn't until October.
singapore.key_development: US10Y surged 6bp to 4.54% after the jobs blowout, extending the dual-headwind past day 72 with no easing catalyst in sight.

semiconductors.summary: SOXX crashed 8.71% as Broadcom's $16B AI chip forecast miss collided with the jobs report, destroying $58B in market cap across the cluster. Only NVDA and LITE reached actionable territory, pulling back to their 50-day moving averages; every other name remains extended or broken below key supports, with no entry until the rate scare subsides.
semiconductors.thesis_status: Structurally bullish, tactically impaired after AVGO-led rout
semiconductors.key_development: SOXX suffered its worst single-day loss of the year (-8.71%) as AVGO's guidance overhang and the 172K payrolls print triggered indiscriminate semis selling.

big_tech.summary: BigTech fared slightly better than Semis but still sold off as rate-hike fears hammered growth multiples. MSFT was downgraded to HOLD after R:R compressed to 0.9:1, and AMZN faces added Prime Day shipping-cost pressure from the Hormuz crisis. GOOG held support near the 50-day, the only name with a remotely workable setup.
big_tech.thesis_status: Mixed — capex confirmed but rate sensitivity rising
big_tech.key_development: MSFT downgraded to HOLD as the jobs data and AVGO miss reset risk appetite; AMZN's Prime Day logistics costs from Hormuz add a company-specific headwind.

interconnected.name: Coherent
interconnected.reason: Directly linked to LITE via NVIDIA's $2B photonics investment — the optics supply-chain lock-in for AI datacenter interconnects.
interconnected.entry_note: Add to watchlist if LITE's Greensboro fab catalyst confirms and COHR pulls back 8-10% from current levels post-semiconductor rout.

interconnected.name: Cadence Design Systems
interconnected.reason: Read-through to ASML/INTC decoupling thesis: US EDA software export restrictions to China threaten semiconductor design flows.
interconnected.entry_note: Watch if China's new supply-chain rules (per Pinsent Masons) force Western EDA firms to curtail China support, potentially compressing CDNS's revenue outlook.

interconnected.name: Marvell Technology
interconnected.reason: NVDA CEO Jensen Huang explicitly named Marvell as a partner in the AI infrastructure buildout, directly adjacent to NVDA's custom networking push.
interconnected.entry_note: Add to watchlist if NVIDIA's next earnings call confirms Marvell's role in Spectrum-X or NVLink switching and MRVL pullbacks 5-7% from current levels.

active_risks: Strait of Hormuz: US-Iran standoff enters day 96, with shipping paralyzed and fertilizer/energy supply chains strained despite WTI easing to $90.54.
US Fed rate hike repricing: May payrolls blowout (172K vs 85K est) has traders fully pricing a quarter-point hike by December, spiking the 10Y to 4.54%.
US tariffs / forced-labor push: Trump proposes 10-12.5% tariffs on 60 nations, raising conflict-of-law risks for multinationals as China retaliates with supply-chain rules (Order No. 834).
EU technology sovereignty—ASML pushback: ASML's CEO warned Brussels that the Commission's new tech sovereignty package, while directionally aligned, risks overreach on export controls.
geopolitical.market_impact: The rate shock hammered growth-sensitive semis (SOXX -8.7%) and BigTech (QQQ -4.1%), while defense-linked PLTR remained resilient. WTI's decline signals that demand-destruction fears now dominate the Hormuz supply premium, compressing energy names.
geopolitical.new_since_yesterday: NFP surged to 172K (est 85K), triggering a full repricing of Fed rate hikes and sending the VIX above 21 for the first time in weeks. Broadcom's AI chip forecast miss from Thursday continued to weigh, turning a single-name event into a sector-wide rout.
geopolitical.portfolio_action: Hold cash, no new entries until VIX stabilizes below 18 and semis find support on volume.

portfolio_snapshot.risk_posture: 13 of 27 names are hard-blocked on technical extension, and the payrolls shock has repriced rate expectations — no entry until the VIX retreats and the semis cluster finds a floor.

Taiwan AI infrastructure partnerships; CoWoS capacity commitments from TSMC; HBM4 roadmap. Direct thesis-confirming event for AI Power Infrastructure cluster.
AI kit, on-device model integration, Vision Pro 2 rumours. Competitive signal for MSFT Copilot and GOOG Gemini on-device.

calibration_insights.confidence_banner: Actionable buckets BUY (n=4) / ACCUMULATE (n=11) spanning chop, trend_up. NOT yet decision-grade — alpha figures are benchmark-relative; single-regime samples carry no out-of-regime evidence.
calibration_insights.note: Block is output-only — assembled post-LLM from signal_evaluation_log outcomes and never injected into the analysis prompt. The underlying lesson stats are separately persisted into report_memory.json under lessons_learned and DO reach the next day's prompt via the memory Layer 3 calibration loop. Treat as a reader-facing self-knowledge summary.
